```
+import { globalStyle, style } from '@vanilla-extract/css'
+
+import { richContent } from './shared.css'
+import { darkNoteTheme, noteTheme, vars } from './vars.css'
+
+const noteBase = style({
+  maxWidth: '65ch',
+  fontSize: vars.typography.fontSizeLarge,
+  lineHeight: '1.8',
+  color: vars.color.text,
+})
+
+export const noteVariant = style([richContent, noteTheme, noteBase])
+export const darkNoteVariant = style([richContent, darkNoteTheme, noteBase])
+
+// ─── Paragraphs (prose-style with text-indent) ──────────
+globalStyle(`${noteBase} .rich-paragraph`, {
+  marginTop: '1.25em',
+  marginBottom: '1.25em',
+  lineHeight: '1.8',
+})
+
+globalStyle(`${noteBase} .rich-paragraph:first-child`, {
+  marginTop: 0,
+  marginBottom: '2rem',
+})
+
+globalStyle(`${noteBase} .rich-paragraph:not(:first-child)`, {
+  textIndent: '2rem',
+})
+
+// Drop cap on first paragraph
+globalStyle(`${noteBase} .rich-paragraph:first-child::first-letter`, {
+  float: 'left',
+  fontSize: '2.4em',
+  marginRight: '0.2em',
+  lineHeight: '1',
+})
+
+globalStyle(`${noteBase} .rich-paragraph:last-child`, {
+  marginBottom: 0,
+})
+
+// ─── Bold uses sans-serif ────────────────────────────────
+globalStyle(`${noteBase} .rich-text-bold`, {
+  fontFamily:

+  color: vars.color.text,
+})
+
```

```
    26	import { ContentEditable } from './ContentEditable'
    27	import { clsx, getVariantClass } from './utils'
    28	
    29	export function RichEditor({
    30	  initialValue,
    31	  onChange,
    32	  variant = 'article',
    33	  colorScheme = 'light',
    34	  placeholder = 'Write something...',
    35	  onSubmit,
    36	  autoFocus = false,
    37	  className,
    38	  contentClassName,
    39	  actions,
    40	  onEditorReady,
    41	  extraNodes,
    42	  rendererConfig,
    43	  debounceMs,
    44	  children,
    45	}: RichEditorProps) {
    46	  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allNodes, ...extraNodes] : allNodes
    47	  const initialConfig = {
    48	    namespace: 'RichEditor',
    49	    theme: editorTheme,
    50	    nodes,
    51	    editable: true,
    52	    onError: (error: Error) => {
    53	      console.error('[RichEditor]', error)
    54	    },
    55	    ...(initialValue ? { editorState: JSON.stringify(initialValue) } : {}),
    56	  }
    57	
    58	  const variantClass = getVariantClass(variant, colorScheme)
    59	
    60	  return (
    61	    <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={colorScheme}>
    62	      <RendererConfigProvider config={rendererConfig} mode="editor">
    63	        <LexicalComposer initialConfig={initialConfig}>
    64	          <div className={clsx('rich-editor', variantClass, className)}>
    65	            <RichTextPlugin
    66	              contentEditable={
    67	                <ContentEditable
    68	                  className={contentClassName}
    69	                  placeholder={placeholder}
    70	                />
    71	              }
    72	              ErrorBoundary={LexicalErrorBoundary}
    73	            />
    74	            <HistoryPlugin />
    75	            <ListPlugin />
    76	            <LinkPlugin />
    77	            <TabIndentationPlugin />
    78	            <TablePlugin />
    79	            <MarkdownShortcutsPlugin />
    80	            <OnChangePlugin onChange={onChange} debounceMs={debounceMs} />
    81	            <SubmitShortcutPlugin onSubmit={onSubmit} />
    82	            <ImagePlugin />
    83	            <KaTeXPlugin />
    84	            <AlertPlugin />
    85	            <MermaidPlugin />
    86	            <ComponentPlugin />
    87	            <AutoLinkPlugin />
    88	            <EditorRefPlugin onEditorReady={onEditorReady} />
    89	            {autoFocus && <AutoFocusPlugin />}
    90	            {children}
```

```
     1	import type { FC, ReactNode } from 'react'
     2	
     3	export interface DialogStackItemProps {
     4	  title: ReactNode
     5	  description?: ReactNode
     6	  content: FC<{ dismiss: () => void }>
     7	  className?: string
     8	  showCloseButton?: boolean
     9	  clickOutsideToDismiss?: boolean
    10	}
    11	
    12	export interface DialogStackItem extends DialogStackItemProps {
    13	  id: string
    14	}
    15	
    16	let stack: DialogStackItem[] = []
    17	let idCounter = 0
    18	const listeners = new Set<() => void>()
    19	
    20	function emit() {
    21	  for (const fn of listeners) fn()
    22	}
    23	
    24	export function subscribe(fn: () => void) {
    25	  listeners.add(fn)
    26	  return () => {
    27	    listeners.delete(fn)
    28	  }
    29	}
    30	
    31	export function getSnapshot(): DialogStackItem[] {
    32	  return stack
    33	}
    34	
    35	export function presentDialog(props: DialogStackItemProps): () => void {
    36	  const id = `dialog-${++idCounter}`
    37	  stack = [...stack, { ...props, id }]
    38	  emit()
    39	  return () => dismissDialog(id)
    40	}
    41	
    42	export function dismissDialog(id: string) {
    43	  stack = stack.filter((item) => item.id !== id)
    44	  emit()
    45	}
    46	
    47	export function dismissTopDialog() {
    48	  stack = stack.slice(0, -1)
    49	  emit()
    50	}
    51	
    52	export function dismissAllDialogs() {
    53	  stack = []
    54	  emit()
    55	}
```

```
     9	/**
    10	 * Sakura (Cherry Blossom) background (WebGPU instanced quads)

    13	 *
    14	 * Notes:
    15	 * - WebGPU does not support point sprites, so we draw instanced quads.
    16	 * - Uses pseudo-3D: particles have a z depth projected to screen.
    17	 */
    18	
    19	const shaderSource = `
    20	struct Uniforms {
    21	  resolutionTime: vec4<f32>,
    22	  petalParams: vec4<f32>,
    23	  colorParams: vec4<f32>,
    24	};
    25	
    26	@group(0) @binding(0) var<uniform> uniforms: Uniforms;
    27	
    28	const PI: f32 = 3.14159265;
    29	
    30	struct VertexInput {
    31	  @location(0) localPos: vec2<f32>,
    32	  @location(1) instancePos: vec2<f32>,
    33	  @location(2) size: f32,
    34	  @location(3) alpha: f32,
    35	  @location(4) seed: f32,
    36	  @location(5) rotation: f32,
    37	  @location(6) blur: f32,
    38	  @location(7) tilt: f32,
    39	};
    40	
    41	struct VertexOutput {
    42	  @builtin(position) Position: vec4<f32>,
    43	  @location(0) localPos: vec2<f32>,
    44	  @location(1) alpha: f32,
    45	  @location(2) seed: f32,
    46	  @location(3) rotation: f32,
    47	  @location(4) blur: f32,
    48	  @location(5) tilt: f32,

    50	
    51	fn hash11(p: f32) -> f32 {
    52	  var x = fract(p * 0.1031);
    53	  x = x * (x + 33.33);
    54	  x = x * (x + x);
    55	  return fract(x);
    56	}
    57	
    58	fn hash21(p: vec2<f32>) -> f32 {
    59	  let h = dot(p, vec2<f32>(127.1, 311.7));
    60	  return fract(sin(h) * 43758.5453);
    61	}
    62	
    63	fn lerp(a: f32, b: f32, t: f32) -> f32 {
```

**Findings**
1. Medium: Potential breaking API change in `@shiro/rich-editor-ui` without compatibility layer. `DialogFlipDirection`, `useDialog`, and `from` support were removed (`packages/rich-editor-ui/src/index.ts:1`, `packages/rich-editor-ui/src/components/dialog/index.tsx:45`, `packages/rich-editor-ui/src/components/dialog/store.ts:3`). If any external consumers rely on these, this is a compile/runtime break and should be versioned/deprecated accordingly.
2. Medium: New `as any` bypass weakens type safety for editor state fixtures in the new preset (`packages/rich-editor-demo/src/fixtures/presets.ts:81`). This can hide malformed node payloads until runtime.
3. Low: Spoiler editor override is globally scoped to any `[contenteditable="true"]` instead of rich-editor scope (`packages/rich-editor/src/styles/shared.css.ts:318`). This can unintentionally affect other editable surfaces that use `.rich-spoiler`.
4. Low: Drop-cap reset for quote content in note style is incomplete. Drop cap adds `marginRight`/`lineHeight` (`packages/rich-editor/src/styles/note.css.ts:33`), but quote reset only clears `float`/`fontSize` (`packages/rich-editor/src/styles/note.css.ts:162`), so spacing artifacts may remain.

**Open Questions / Assumptions**
1. I assumed `packages/rich-editor-ui` is a public/shared package; if it is strictly internal, finding #1 severity is lower.
2. I assumed the global spoiler selector was unintended; if this is deliberate cross-editor behavior, finding #3 can be ignored.
